title: Phlix Hub settings
description: Schema describing the editable settings for the Phlix Hub. The hub is the identity and directory service for a Phlix estate: it enrolls servers, mints and renews the JWTs those servers authenticate with, brokers the reverse-tunnel relay, and owns cross-server federation and sharing. It does NOT scan libraries or transcode media — that is the server's job. Property keys are the literal dotted setting keys and MUST match Phlix\Hub\Hub\HubSettingsRepository::ALLOWED_KEYS, which is the writable allow-list the hub settings controller enumerates; the schema supplies only the render metadata for those keys. Each key is a dotted path into phlix-hub/config/<file>.php (first segment = flat config file name) and must resolve to a non-null default. Boot-bound infrastructure and secrets — the hub JWT secret, relay TLS material, the hub base/public domain baked into issued enrollment JWTs, ACME/TLS provisioning toggles, host/port/worker counts, and Sonarr/Radarr API keys — are deliberately excluded and are enumerated in HubSettingsRepository::DENIED_KEYS.

server.enrollment_ttl.description: How long a server enrollment token remains valid after being issued, in seconds.
server.enrollment_ttl.label: Enrollment token TTL
server.enrollment_ttl.helpText: When a server enrolls with this hub it receives a signed JSON Web Token valid for this many seconds; after that it must renew. The shipped value is 604800 seconds (7 days). Longer values mean fewer renewal round-trips but widen the window in which a leaked enrollment token could be replayed. The value is read each time a token is minted, so a change applies to the next enrollment without a restart — tokens already issued keep the lifetime they were minted with.

auth.access_ttl.description: Hub JWT access-token lifetime in seconds.
auth.access_ttl.label: Access token TTL
auth.access_ttl.helpText: How many seconds a hub access token stays valid before the client must exchange its refresh token for a new one. Shorter lifetimes shrink the damage window of a stolen token at the cost of more refresh calls. The shipped value is 3600 seconds (1 hour). The value is resolved on every token mint, so a change applies to newly issued tokens immediately; tokens already in circulation keep their original expiry.

auth.refresh_ttl.label: Refresh token TTL
auth.refresh_ttl.helpText: The maximum age of a refresh token before it can no longer be exchanged for a new access token and the user must sign in again. This should be substantially longer than the access token TTL. The shipped value is 604800 seconds (7 days). The value is resolved on every token mint, so a change applies to newly issued tokens immediately.
